commands: node scripts/run-agent-evidence.js --validate agent/evidence/windows agent/evidence/linux agent/evidence/macos agent/evidence/codex agent/evidence/claude agent/evidence/cross-host
validation.summary: Syntax check covers 114 files; isolated Windows smoke passes; npm payload is bounded to 52 files; both generated host artifacts and archives share build 48cc9c90; all 28 evidence cells validate with zero diagnostics; diff whitespace check passes.

The full suite ran 313 tests with 311 passing, one existing POSIX skip, and one temporary test-lock timeout caused by a prior externally terminated test process. The affected global-runtime suite was rerun immediately and passes 24/24; no assertion regression remains, but the original full-suite process exit is retained as nonzero evidence.
The evidence index maps every listed cell to its exact build identity and cannot carry a broad release field; final surface and policy audits pass.

review.evidence: Documentation and index distinguish the current 48cc9c90 candidate from superseded core/Codex evidence. Current Claude routing failed before dispatch, Codex lifecycle remains an older failed cell, cross-host lifecycle is not executed, and macOS lacks a native executor; none is presented as host readiness.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Automated contracts, smoke, package build, real stdio/CLI suites, deterministic artifact tests, exact evidence validation, and the final audit provide the implementation evidence. Host/OS/lifecycle gaps remain explicit in agent/evidence/index.json and are not widened into a release claim.

recovery: Technical implementation is complete by explicit user direction. Preserve the exact evidence index and task lifecycles; defer workflow-runtime reconciliation as external baseline maintenance, without hand-editing workflow-runtime.json or claiming an Implement-to-Review transition.
